name: Larry Willis
abstract: Lawrence Elliott Willis (December 20, 1942 – September 29, 2019) was an American jazz pianist and composer. He performed in a wide range of styles, including jazz fusion, Afro-Cuban jazz, bebop, and avant-garde.

concerts.label: An Evening with Blood, Sweat and Tears

works.t: You've Made Me So Very Happy

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Wayne Shorter and Herbie Hancock

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: For years I’ve had the privilege to play with the great Larry Willis, the great pianist, and he’s a dear friend of mine. ... at least 15 years we’ve been playing a lot, whether it’s his band or my band or whatever collaborations, or just on gigs together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: And the writers in the band were great, Larry Willis, Tony Klatka, they were great writers, they would write material for the band... I came in at the very tail end of the success you know. I was on salary. I didn’t share in any of the revenues, shall we say, in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: We brought in Larry Willis on piano, Earl May on drums, and the drummer at that time was Dick Berk.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: There was "Jackknife" and there was another one, two records with him. One with Larry Willis and…
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Booker, Larry Willis, and Jimmy Cobb, swingin’ record. And Steve. Steve is on a couple tracks.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: Larry Willis was on the band for a while. ... Larry was, actually I think Larry recommended me for the job, because he was on there before me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995
